title: Freatulina regia
intro:
There is no information about the plant Freatulina regia, however, based on the name, it seems to be a possible misspelling of the plant Fritillaria regina or possibly Fritillaria meleagris's relative Fritillaria regeliana or a species of Fritillaria that is also known as  Fritillaria regia which is a bulbous perennial plant. 

Fritillaria regia, a plant species within the Fritillaria genus, is a plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. 

This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain diseases.

The bioactive constituents of Fritillaria regia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. 

Fritillaria regia is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and other pharmaceutical products.
